g.text: epicd — architecture class skeleton (3 planes + 2 contracts + RefineStrategy registry)
Plane 1 · core state machine (pure, canonical, slowest-changing)
Authoring extension · RefineStrategy registry (kind selects strategy)
phase : string '' bare phase name — SOLE persisted progress axis (inert `state` DELETED)
labels : string[] '' kind:feature / kind:chore / kind:epic live here
step(task : Task, artifacts) : RefineStep '' PURE, re-entrant; cursor DERIVED (project), not a param
label(state, role, plane) : string '' PURE, one-way, boundary-only
byPipeline : Map<pipeline_id, {columns, sort, collapsed, icon, title}>
scan/dispatch exist today but are TEST-ONLY (src/engine/*).
Handlers are the extension point (Plane-2 wiring + authoring strategies).
FOUR-AXIS (2026-07-04 FINAL): per-task stores pipeline_id + bare phase.
turn = pipelineDef[phase].actor (pipeline-data, NOT a field) · role from tree · claim runtime.
pick-up predicate = pipelineDef[phase].actor==machine ∧ no live claim.
status = label(role,phase) display only. Inert engine `state` DELETED (E1/BACK-601).
BOUNDARY = intent vs mechanism. Strategy decides WHAT (RefineStep);
RefiningHandler/driver decides HOW (which Claude Code invocation, worktree,
retry) and PERFORMS all side effects. Strategy sees only DATA (task+artifacts
+cursor) — no session handles/paths — so it unit-tests without a harness.
Authoring pipeline stays MINIMAL: Draft→Refining→Backlog. Fine cycle progress
is strategy-internal (cursor), surfaced to UI as a Coordinator runtime-fact
(projection), NOT a pipeline state. Adding kind:inquiry = register a strategy;
state machine & driver unchanged (pipeline-as-data / ADR-011 D-2 kept clean).
ANTI-CORRUPTION SEAM. Contract SHAPE is stable across soak↔engine;
only the adapter behind it swaps. Claim is a RUNTIME FACT, never a
persisted phase. Claim (durable + timestamped) IS the checkout record:
actor(phase)=machine ∧ live claim ⇒ 🤖 active ; ∧ no claim ⇒ ⏳ queued ;
∧ claim timed out ⇒ ⚠️ stale/orphaned. (no persisted in-progress needed)
ONE runtime-fact channel for BOTH planes: execution claims AND authoring
refine progress (e.g. "Refining · review cycle 2") ride here as projection.
Plane 1 owns the ONLY canonical vocabulary.
Planes 2 & 3 PROJECT from it; never define states.
Coupling only via the two <<contract>> seams (read-only, one-way).
UI/config strings must NEVER feed back into engine logic.
